evidence: The paper explicitly applies "a permutation based method that exploits the distribution of measured importance for each variable in a non-informative setting" with 10000 permutations. No mention of Gini, impurity, MDI, or variance importance. The random forest analysis is framed as "additional clinical validation of the clusters" rather than a primary biomarker discovery effort. Core conclusions focus on identifying seven distinct treatment response classes and demonstrating their generalizability across samples. Predictive validity is corroborated through classification accuracy on held-out validation samples (75.0-95.2%).
